role.title: EVP / SVP, Business Affairs
role.organization: Studio / Network / Platform or Production Company
role.relevance_to_agent: The most senior deal strategist. Sets deal parameters, approves above-threshold terms, and makes the final call on whether issues are deal-breakers or acceptable risks. Has seen hundreds of deals and knows which issues actually matter in practice vs. which are theoretical. The Risk Strategist's primary model.
role.analysis_lens: What must change before I sign? What can I live with? What's the priority order? Where do I have leverage and where don't I?

role.title: General Counsel / Chief Legal Officer
role.organization: Studio / Network / Platform or Production Company
role.relevance_to_agent: Ultimate authority on legal risk tolerance. Approves non-standard terms, assesses litigation probability, and evaluates enterprise-level risk. The risk calibration model.
role.analysis_lens: What is our total risk exposure? Which provisions create litigation or regulatory enforcement risk? Is this deal within our risk tolerance?

role.title: Partner, Entertainment / Media Practice (Outside Counsel)
role.relevance_to_agent: Develops transaction structure, advises on strategy, and provides industry benchmarking. Brings cross-deal pattern recognition and knows what terms are actually achievable in the current market. The market-context model.
role.analysis_lens: How does this deal compare to market? Which terms are negotiable in the current environment? What is the opposing side likely to concede vs. hold firm on?

role.title: Entertainment Attorney (Talent-Side, Strategic)
role.relevance_to_agent: The talent-side strategic counterpart. Knows which protections are non-negotiable for their client, which are aspirational, and where creative compromises exist. Provides the adversarial perspective — what will the other side fight for?
role.analysis_lens: If I'm on the other side, which of these findings would I push back on? Which are we likely to win? Which require creative structuring rather than a direct demand?
